source_name: Code4rena Dyad Findings and Analysis Report (2024-04)
finding_title: No incentive to liquidate when CR <= 1 as asset received < dyad burned
summary: Right now there are no incentives to liquidate a position with a CR<1, as the liquidator will have to burn the full borrowed amount, and will get the full collateral.
root_cause: liquidation incentive math can make liquidators receive less value than the debt burned, so unhealthy positions are economically ignored
broken_invariants: the vault path must not allow the condition described by "No incentive to liquidate when CR <= 1 as asset received < dyad burned" to change user balances, collateralization, or protocol availability unexpectedly
oracle, TVL, and collateral valuation must use correctly scaled, fresh, and manipulation-resistant inputs
reward and distribution accounting must preserve each participant’s accrued value across claims, transfers, and configuration changes
attack_preconditions: the affected component is deployed or configured in the way described by the final report
the report-described user, keeper, vault, connector, oracle, or liquidation flow is reachable in the audited code version
attacker_capability: external user, keeper, liquidator, vault participant, or protocol-integrated actor able to trigger the affected flow
trigger_actions: execute the report-described function sequence or state transition
observe mispricing, accounting drift, value loss, fund lock, or denial of service

impact.description: Right now there are no incentives to liquidate a position with a CR<1, as the liquidator will have to burn the full borrowed amount, and will get the full collateral.

why_not_higher: final C4 severity is Medium; exploitability, affected value, or preconditions are bounded relative to High
why_not_lower: included as a validated High/Medium C4 finding with public report evidence and affected code/finding references
common_false_positive_angles: verify the same component and configuration are in scope
check whether the issue is reachable by an untrusted or economically motivated actor
confirm the impact is more than a view-only mismatch, dust loss, or privileged-only misconfiguration
mitigations: Refactor the liquidation calculation, to allow liquidator to repay the debt and still get a reward out of this.
